title: pleopeltis glossipes
intro: Pleopeltis glossipes, also known as the glossyleaved polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Pleopeltis glossipes include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its use in traditional medicine to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Pleopeltis glossipes can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically to aid in the healing process.
